title: 3 Legal Considerations
body_text:
3	Legal Considerations
3.1	CPA Rules
Payments Canada, formerly the Canadian Payment Association (CPA), develops and implements the rules that apply to the clearing and settlement of different types of payments between its member financial institutions. Refer to the Payments Canada website for more information on CPA Rules.
CPA Rule A4, Returned and Redirected Items â
Outlines procedures, time frames and responsibilities related to the dishonour and return of cheques and other payment items.
CPA Rule G8, Procedures/Criteria Pertaining to the Encashment of Receiver General Warrants for Non-Customers â
Provides encashment and identification requirements for Receiver General Warrants for non-customers. When these procedures are followed, the federal government indemnifies financial institutions against fraud losses arising from the encashment of Receiver General Warrants up to $1,750.
3.2	Bills of Exchange Act
The
Bills of Exchange Act (BEA)
governs the form of and dealings with financial instruments such as bills of exchange, cheques, and promissory notes. The
BEA
regulates financial issues such as the time permitted for the dishonour and return of cheques, forged endorsements, and the rights and powers of the bill of exchange holder.
The
BEA
states that âan Official Image of an eligible bill may be dealt with and used for all purposes as though it were the eligible billâ.
END
